The Frontend Dev Forced to Eat Mango (1): Meeting MongoDB and Installing It
The Frontend Dev Forced to Eat Mango

Time for another learning log, and this time it's all about MongoDB. Since "Mongo" sounds a lot like "mango" in Chinese, I named this series "The Fullstack Dev Forced to Eat Mango." In this first post I'll cover what MongoDB actually is, how it differs from SQL, what BSON is, and how to install and run it on both Mac and Windows.

Most of us are used to running `npm install` when restoring a project, but since npm 5.7.1 there's been a recommendation to use `npm ci` instead. This post walks through why, and when each one is the right call.

Sending Gmail with nodemailer After Google Killed Password Auth (OAuth 2.0)

This time I want to add an extra note about nodemailer. Google no longer lets you send Gmail messages with just a username and password, so I'm writing down how to set up Express + OAuth 2.0 + nodemailer + Gmail instead. It's a quick walkthrough — building the OAuth client, grabbing the refresh and access tokens, and wiring them into nodemailer.
